mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive monthly changes in retail sales and housing starts yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output that generates short-term positive S&P 500 returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when the federal funds rate remains unchanged for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 50% of months where CPI exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields negative forward returns in > 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening residential investment that drags on near-term economic momentum and produces negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Housing start declines greater than 5 percent fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, with positive returns occurring in > 60% of cases.
